perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the second anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date, unless extended by mutual written agreement. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the fifth anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement terminates on the second anniversary. The modified version now allows for an extension by mutual written agreement, while simultaneously extending the confidentiality obligations to a fifth anniversary, creating a contradiction. The termination date now can be extended, but the confidentiality is automatically extended, even without extending the actual agreement. This contradiction can occur if an extension isn't explicitly written out, and it creates confusion in the text of section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination of Discussions. This Agreement and all rights and obligations hereunder shall terminate on the second anniversary of the Effective Date, unless extended by mutual written agreement. Promptly upon notice from either party that it does not wish to proceed with the Transaction, each party shall return to the other party or destroy all copies of the Evaluation Material (including all documents based thereon) in its possession or in the possession of its Representatives, except that outside counsel to the receiving party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material for archival purposes and solely to comply with applicable law, rule or regulation. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by its obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder for a period ending on the fifth anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text stipulates that the agreement terminates on the second anniversary of the Effective Date. However, the changed version allows for an extension of the agreement by mutual written agreement, while simultaneously extending the confidentiality obligations to a fifth anniversary, regardless of whether the agreement itself is extended. This creates a scenario where the confidentiality obligations extend beyond the potential termination of the agreement, leading to a contradiction. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text, now contains inconsistent terms regarding the duration of the overall agreement versus the confidentiality obligations, and can be seen as an Ambiguity - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The term “definitive agreement” does not include a term sheet or any other preliminary written agreement. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The term “definitive agreement” includes any written and signed document outlining the core terms of the transaction. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original states a 'definitive agreement' excludes a term sheet. The edited version includes any written and signed document outlining core terms. This now makes the obligation in section 8 ambiguous, as it now can include a term sheet. This leads to in-text contradiction in the language of section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Definitive Agreements. Unless and until a final definitive agreement, regarding the Transaction has been executed, no contract or agreement with respect to the Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties and neither party will be under any legal obligation whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The term “definitive agreement” includes any written and signed document outlining the core terms of the transaction. Each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to provide or not provide Evaluation Material under this Agreement, to reject any and all proposals with regard to the Transaction and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes term sheets from the definition of 'definitive agreement,' while the changed text includes any written and signed document outlining core terms, which could encompass a term sheet. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Because the meaning of 'definitive agreement' is changed to include term sheets, this causes an ambiguity and contradiction as to the obligations under section 8, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement may be made electronically, verified by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, all modifications had to be in writing and signed. Now, modifications can be done electronically and only require verification. It introduces a contradiction if the parties had established a process for wet-ink signatures and are only now told they can simply be verified. The change is in section 9, and it can be contradicted against the original language itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Entire Agreement. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement with respect to the Evaluation Material disclosed hereunder and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements concerning such Evaluation Material, whether written or oral. All additions or modifications to this Agreement may be made electronically, verified by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text allows modifications to be made electronically and verified by an authorized representative. This directly contradicts the original requirement of written and signed modifications, as electronic verification is not equivalent to a written signature. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists within the document, specifically in section 9, as the original and changed text are located in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Relief. Each party agrees that money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Relief. Each party agrees that money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach, where available. Such remedies shall be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a material breach of this Agreement, unless otherwise agreed to in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The change shifts from specific performance as a guaranteed right to 'seek specific performance...where available.' This introduces ambiguity and depends on judicial discretion. Furthermore, the change specifies exclusive remedies for 'material breach'. This is contradictory, because it excludes all remedies for non-material breaches. The contradiction is found in section 12 in its entirety.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Relief. Each party agrees that money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, and that the other party is entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach, where available. Such remedies shall be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a material breach of this Agreement, unless otherwise agreed to in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text guaranteed specific performance as a remedy for any breach. The changed text modifies this to 'seek specific performance...where available,' making it subject to judicial discretion. More critically, it specifies that remedies are exclusive only for a 'material breach.' This creates a direct contradiction because it implies that there are no remedies for non-material breaches, which contradicts the overall intent of section 12, the contradicted text which guarantees remedies. Thus, based on the explanation provided and the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, a strong contradiction exists.
